checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=50159530.24 (2011-06-30)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=101439692.72 (2015-06-30)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=82123898.77 (2014-06-30) vs 60156871.83 (2013-06-30), a 36.5% YoY increase, while Revenues grew 23.7% (588456701.10 vs 475569664.76 for FY2014 vs FY2013)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Revenues=698074839.24 (2014-07-01 to 2015-06-30) grew only 18.6% vs Goodwill growth of 142.7%
PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet=381936423.34 (2014-06-30) rising to 611101067.31 (2015-06-30), a 60% increase vs 18.6% revenue growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=23777398.25 (2013-07-01 to 2014-06-30); NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=54494811.55 (2013-07-01 to 2014-06-30); DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization=50047932.01 (2013-07-01 to 2014-06-30) implying NI+D&A of ~73.8M vs OCF of 54.5M, a ~19.3M shortfall

NetIncomeLoss=28842718.00 (2014-07-01 to 2015-06-30); NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=34537327.77 (2014-07-01 to 2015-06-30); DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization=58369750.15 (2014-07-01 to 2015-06-30) implying NI+D&A of ~87.2M vs OCF of only 34.5M, a ~52.7M shortfall that widened from the prior year

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold data only provided through FY2011 (value 2741131.57 for 2010-07-01 to 2011-06-30); no COGS data disclosed for FY2012-FY2015 periods despite Revenues data existing through FY2015 (698074839.24)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2011 income statement / subsequent filings lack COGS tag

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss annual values rose steadily: 21129265.21 (FY2013), 23777398.25 (FY2014), 28842718.00 (FY2015)
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities annual values declined over the same period: 67198410.26 (FY2013), 54494811.55 (FY2014), 34537327.77 (FY2015)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=809862.17 (2011-06-30)
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=776537.70 (2015-06-30)

AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 50159530.24 (2011-06-30) to 101439692.72 (2015-06-30), a 102% increase, while the allowance balance decreased slightly (-4%), dropping allowance-to-AR coverage from ~1.6% to ~0.77%

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date 2015-03-30 following the original 10-K filed 2014-09-15 for FY2014, an amendment roughly 6.5 months after the original annual report
Two 10-Q/A amendments filed on the same date 2012-05-25 (accessions 0001008886-12-000085 and 0001008886-12-000086), restating quarterly filings for periods ended 2011-09-30 and 2011-12-31

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, Goodwill, PropertyPlantAndEquipmentNet, and cash flow statement items spanning FY2010 through FY2015 (e.g., Revenues=409672458.88 (2009-07-01 to 2010-06-30) through Revenues=698074839.24 (2014-07-01 to 2015-06-30))

However, CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold, Liabilities (total), and LiabilitiesCurrent/AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent data are sparse or missing for several periods (e.g., Liabilities only reported for 4 dates in 2011-2013, none in FY2014-2015), limiting some analyses
evidence.location: 10-Q filings with sparse Liabilities tag coverage

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition combined with insufficient growth in bad-debt reserves, resulting in receivables and revenue that outpace underlying cash collections; the allowance-to-receivables coverage ratio fell from ~1.6% (2011) to ~0.77% (2015) even as receivables more than doubled, which could mask deteriorating collectability and overstate net receivables and reported revenue/earnings.

Earnings are increasingly decoupled from operating cash generation - net income plus D&A substantially exceeds actual operating cash flow, with the gap widening sharply in FY2015 (~52.7M shortfall vs ~19.3M in FY2014). This pattern is consistent with revenue being recognized ahead of cash collection (accrual-heavy earnings) or working-capital-related earnings management, rather than a one-time timing issue.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities declined year over year: 67198410.26 (FY2013), 54494811.55 (FY2014), 34537327.77 (FY2015) despite rising NetIncomeLoss

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Rapid, acquisition-driven growth in goodwill (142.7% YoY in FY2015) and net PP&E (60% YoY) far outpacing revenue growth (18.6%) raises the risk of purchase-price allocation aggressiveness, delayed/insufficient impairment testing, or capitalization of costs that should be expensed, which would overstate non-current assets and understate expenses/impairment charges.

top_signals: Accounts receivable growth (102% over FY2011-FY2015) persistently outpacing revenue growth (58.5% over same period), implying rising DSO from ~41 to ~53 days
Widening gap between net income (+D&A) and operating cash flow, particularly a ~52.7M shortfall in FY2015 versus ~19.3M in FY2014, indicating deteriorating earnings quality/cash conversion
Declining allowance-for-doubtful-accounts coverage ratio (from ~1.6% to ~0.77% of receivables) despite receivables more than doubling
Goodwill and PP&E growing far faster than revenue (142.7% and 60% vs 18.6% revenue growth in FY2015), raising acquisition-accounting and impairment risk
Presence of a 10-K/A filed 2015-03-30 amending the FY2014 10-K, plus two 10-Q/A amendments filed simultaneously in 2012, suggesting historical restatement activity
